facts.subject: user_editor
facts.object: NeoVim (switched last month — should be marked superseded)

facts.subject: user_os
facts.object: macOS (switched in March — should be marked superseded)

must_preserve: User's CURRENT editor is Helix (NOT NeoVim — that's superseded)
User's CURRENT OS is Asahi Linux on M1 (NOT macOS — that's superseded)
Both corrections supersede prior memory; old facts must be marked invalid
may_drop: the discussion of V1.6/V1.7 features for surfacing corrections
the meta-discussion about backend vs UI
expected_failure_modes: THE MOST IMPORTANT TEST: compaction MUST emit the new facts and MUST mark the old ones invalid. If compaction emits BOTH 'user uses NeoVim' AND 'user uses Helix' as parallel facts, that's a contradiction-detection failure. This is the headline V1.6 capability test.
If the old facts are dropped but new not emitted, that's recall failure
If only new facts are emitted (dropping old), that's acceptable but loses the 'switched in March' temporal context
